summary: H6 is the riskiest leg — 5,356 ln across 13 files, 19 db.fn.now() violations in runSchedule.js (P1 gap), NO scheduler golden-master characterization suite yet (S8 BLOCKING gate). H2/H3/H4 are complete (entry-gate cleared). Entry blocker: write the golden-master BEFORE any extraction work begins.
risk_flags: P1-VIOLATION: runSchedule.js uses db.fn.now() / trx.fn.now() for updated_at in 19 places (lines 404, 789, 804, 912-913, 1241, 1281, 1375, 1399, 1428, 1453, 1476, 1486, 1532, 1539). ADR-0003 requires new Date(). The classifier grep could not see this — it is inside the body of a 2,309-ln file. This must be fixed in the KnexScheduleRepository adapter, not carried forward.

S8-BLOCKING: No scheduler golden-master characterization suite exists in tests/characterization/. The DESIGN §3 extraction gate is an unconditional BLOCK: scheduler slice may not be merged without characterization tests green before AND after. This is the single most important pre-work item for the entire leg.

S4/S6-TRIGGER-SEAM: scheduleQueue.js (not the scheduler core) owns the mutation→schedule trigger. runSchedule.js itself does NOT call enqueueScheduleRun from within the scheduler body — S4/S6 are currently preserved. The extraction must maintain this: RunScheduleCommand must not re-import scheduleQueue. The facade.js wiring must preserve the trigger path through the existing task/facade.js enqueueScheduleRun wrapper.

DEEP-COUPLING-TASK-CONTROLLER: runSchedule.js requires('../controllers/task.controller') for rowToTask/buildSourceMap/taskToRow (line 92-95). This is a direct controller dependency that must be resolved via SchedulerTaskProvider adapter over the task facade — a non-trivial seam to cut.

DEAD-CODE-CONFIRMED: dateHelpers.js, dependencyHelpers.js, locationHelpers.js, timeBlockHelpers.js are all 4-line stubs (likely re-export shims). unifiedSchedule.js is 1 line. These are proxy shims for the real code inside unifiedScheduleV2.js — the actual helpers are inlined or imported elsewhere. Must be inventoried before the extraction.

ENTRY-GATE-GAP-IN-ROADMAP: JUGGLER-HEX-ROADMAP.md version header still says 'leg/juggler-hex-h1-weather @ 2026-06-09' and H2/H3/H4 lack COMPLETE blocks despite git log confirming all three merged (commits bc6e437, 1ac024f, ba8d6ca). The roadmap header/phase docs need updating as part of H6 leg setup.

42-DB-TOUCHPOINTS: The W1 baseline (ARCH-REVIEW) counted 42 DB touchpoints in the scheduler. The KnexScheduleRepository adapter must encapsulate all of them. The characterization suite must pin the current write pattern before the adapter is introduced.

prior_decisions.summary: Scheduler slice may not merge without characterization tests (golden-master over real fixtures) proving pure core reproduces current behavior bit-for-bit before any refactor. This is S8. Non-negotiable — BLOCK.
Scheduler is migrated LAST. The Jan-2026 recommendation of 'scheduler first' is explicitly reversed. This is now H6, gated on H0-H5 all merged.

All Knex*Repository adapters write created_at/updated_at with new Date(), never db.fn.now(). Veto recorded 2026-05-12 — circular-JSON serialization failure. runSchedule.js currently has 19 fn.now() calls that must move into the KnexScheduleRepository and be corrected to new Date().
lib/events adopted as TaskEventPort bus (Task slice publishes mutation events; Scheduler slice subscribes). Direct facade trigger kept until scheduler slice lands. Do not delete lib/events.
lib-db is the route for all Knex*Repository adapters; src/db.js singleton migrated per-slice. KnexScheduleRepository must use lib-db, not src/db.js.

S1 most-constrained→least (ConstraintSolver ordering); S2 Deadlines>dependencies>preferences (severity comparator); S3 recurring instances scheduled same-day as recurrence fires; S4 scheduler triggered by user/MCP mutation only (never self-triggers); S5 delta-writes only (writeChanged — only changed tasks written); S6 no cascading scheduler calls; S7 exact task-type terms via closed-enum VOs; S8 characterization tests gate the merge; P1 new Date() not db.fn.now() on repos.

MCP adapter and HTTP adapter enter via the same facade. Scheduler subscribes to Task mutation events (lib-events). The trigger path must be identical for both driving adapters and the scheduler must never self-trigger.

behavior_contract: S1: ConstraintSolver.solve() orders tasks most-constrained → least-constrained. The slack sort (slack asc, pri asc, dur desc, id) in unifiedScheduleV2.js is the implementation. Characterization must snapshot this ordering for mixed-constraint fixtures.

S2: Severity hierarchy — fixed/immovable > overdue (slack=0, P1-boosted) > explicit/implied deadline (sorted by slack) > free/backlog (slack=Infinity). Deadlines always trump priority as the primary sort key.

S3: Recurring instances are placed ONLY on the same day as their recurrence rule fires. No roll-forward to adjacent days if the assigned day is full — instance goes to unscheduled/issues (SCHEDULER.md §8 Rule 2). Exception: timesPerCycle/flex-day templates may slide within the cycle window by design.

S4: Scheduler is triggered only via enqueueScheduleRun from mutation controllers (or MCP). runSchedule.js body does not call enqueueScheduleRun. scheduleQueue.js owns the trigger. The extracted RunScheduleCommand must not import scheduleQueue.
S5: Only tasks whose scheduled_at actually changed are written back (delta-write). Unchanged tasks receive no DB update. Characterization test must count DB writes = number of changed tasks only.
S6: No cascading scheduler calls from within the scheduler. RunScheduleCommand completes, emits SSE, and returns. No re-enqueue inside the scheduler body.
S7: Task-type terms are exact closed-enum strings: 'one-off', 'chain member', 'recurring instance', 'split chunk'. PlacementMode VO enforces these — no unknown strings.
P1: All Knex*Repository adapter writes use new Date() for created_at/updated_at, never db.fn.now() or trx.fn.now(). KnexScheduleRepository must fix the 19 current violations in runSchedule.js.
IDEMPOTENCE: Back-to-back scheduler runs on stable input produce zero DB changes (second run's pendingUpdates length = 0). Characterization must assert this.

DELTA-WRITE-COUNT: The number of DB updates per run must equal the number of tasks whose placement changed. The existing 'minimal-diff: skip write when scheduled_at unchanged' test in schedulerPersistIntegration.test.js is the seed; golden-master must pin this at the full-run level.
WEATHER-FAIL-OPEN: weatherOk() returns true when no weather data exists for a candidate slot — slot is never blocked by missing data. This fail-open rule must survive extraction.
PAST-DUE-NO-ROLLFORWARD: Non-recurring past-due tasks are P1-boosted and rescheduled within existing constraints. Recurring past-due instances are NOT rolled forward — they go to unscheduled/issues.

OCCUPANCY-GRID: dayOcc prevents overlaps during placement. No post-placement pile-up resolution. Each phase respects all prior phases' placements. Zero overlaps invariant (tested by Group 70 fuzz test in schedulerScenarios).
SYNC-LOCK: One scheduler run per user at a time via sync_locks. scheduleQueue.js owns the DB-claim mechanism. This must remain in the infrastructure adapter layer, not the pure core.
ROLLING-ANCHOR: rolling tasks use rolling_anchor for spacing; backfill path in runSchedule.js handles legacy rows with null rolling_anchor. Must be preserved in the adapter.
characterization_targets: GOLDEN-MASTER-CORE [MISSING — BLOCKING]: tests/characterization/scheduler/goldenMaster.h6.test.js — Feed fixed task/calendar fixtures through the CURRENT unifiedScheduleV2 (pre-extraction), snapshot {dayPlacements, unplaced, score, slackByTaskId}. Then assert the extracted ConstraintSolver/ScoreEngine/ConflictResolver reproduce the same output bit-for-bit. Must be green BEFORE extraction begins.

S1-ORDERING [partial — schedulerRules Group 2, schedulerScenarios S3/S4]: schedulerRules Group 2 covers deadline-governs-priority. schedulerScenarios S3 (dep chain A→B→C ordering), S4b (same-day dep placement) are close but not an explicit most-constrained-to-least ordering snapshot. Golden-master must add a fixture with mixed-constraint tasks and pin the full sorted order.

S2-SEVERITY [partial — schedulerRules Group 2, schedulerScenarios PDS-1/PDS-2]: Deadline-vs-priority ordering tested. Need a fixture that pins the exact severity comparator result (fixed > overdue > deadline > free) as a golden-master snapshot, not just a placement assertion.

S3-SAME-DAY-RECURRENCE [gap]: No existing test explicitly asserts that a recurring instance is placed ONLY on its assigned day and never rolled to adjacent days. schedulerScenarios Group 19 has a test for past recurring tasks being skipped, but no golden-master for same-day placement of future recurrings. Must add.

S4-NO-SELF-TRIGGER [gap]: No existing test asserts that the scheduler core (unifiedScheduleV2) does not import or call scheduleQueue/enqueueScheduleRun. This is a structural assertion — a static require-graph check — that must be added to the golden-master harness. Verify: `require.resolve('./scheduleQueue')` is NOT in unifiedScheduleV2's closure.

S5-DELTA-WRITE-COUNT [partial — schedulerPersistIntegration 'minimal-diff' test line 203]: The 'skip write when scheduled_at unchanged' test covers the single-task case. Golden-master must assert write count = changed tasks at the full multi-task run level (integration test against test-bed 3407).

S6-NO-CASCADE [gap]: No test asserts zero re-entries into the scheduler during a run. Must add a test that confirms RunScheduleCommand does not call enqueueScheduleRun or runScheduleAndPersist recursively. A spy/mock on scheduleQueue.enqueueScheduleRun must remain uncalled across a full scheduler run.

P1-NEW-DATE [gap — ADR-0003]: No existing adapter unit test asserts JS Date (not Knex raw) is passed for updated_at writes. Must add to KnexScheduleRepository unit test. The 19 fn.now() violations in runSchedule.js are the items to fix in the adapter.

IDEMPOTENCE [partial — schedulerIntegration line 203 IDEM-1/IDEM-2 in SCHEDULER.md]: schedulerPersistIntegration has a single idempotence test. Golden-master must add a multi-task idempotence fixture that captures the exact updated count on run-2 = 0.

SCORE-ENGINE [gap]: scoreSchedule.js is 181 ln of pure functions. No characterization test snapshots score output against a fixed fixture. ScoreEngine extraction from unifiedScheduleV2 must be pinned by a fixture that captures {total, breakdown} before extraction and asserts identity after.

WEATHER-CONSTRAINT [partial — SCHEDULER.md §Weather, no test found]: The weatherOk fail-open rule must be asserted in the characterization suite with a fixture that has no weather data and verifies the task IS placed (not blocked).

ambiguities.question: H2/H3/H4 completion status in JUGGLER-HEX-ROADMAP.md: The roadmap header still reads 'leg/juggler-hex-h1-weather @ 2026-06-09' and H2/H3/H4 phases lack COMPLETE blocks. Git log confirms all three merged (H2: bc6e437, H3: 1ac024f, H4: ba8d6ca). Should the roadmap doc be updated as H6 leg setup work (part of this leg's W0), or was it intentionally left for a separate chore?
ambiguities.why_blocking: The H6 entry gate explicitly requires 'all prior phases merged'. If the roadmap is the authoritative state tracker, Kermit may reject the leg as not yet entry-gate-cleared until the doc is updated. Clarify before WBS finalization.

ambiguities.question: 4-line stub files (dateHelpers.js, dependencyHelpers.js, locationHelpers.js, timeBlockHelpers.js) and the 1-line unifiedSchedule.js: are these re-export shims for backward compatibility with old import paths, or dead stubs to be deleted in H7?
ambiguities.why_blocking: If they are backward-compat shims, the extraction must maintain them. If they are dead stubs, the golden-master must not import them. The extraction WBS must account for which callers (if any) still use these paths.

ambiguities.question: scheduleQueue.js entry gate for the trigger seam: The task facade (slices/task/facade.js) already has enqueueScheduleRun wired (line 84, 136-144). Does H6 adopt lib-events (the S4 seam) as the trigger, or does it keep the existing direct facade→scheduleQueue call? The DESIGN §5 says 'until the scheduler slice lands, keep the direct facade call' — but now the scheduler IS landing. Must lib-events be fully wired before H6 can merge?
ambiguities.why_blocking: If lib-events must be the trigger for H6 exit, that is additional scope. If the direct facade call is acceptable for H6 with lib-events as a follow-on, the WBS is smaller. DESIGN §5 implies lib-events is the target but the 'until it lands' clause is ambiguous.

edge_cases: 19 fn.now() violations in runSchedule.js must all migrate to new Date() in KnexScheduleRepository — none can be left in the pure core or RunScheduleCommand.

runSchedule.js requires task.controller directly (rowToTask, buildSourceMap, taskToRow). This dependency must be cut via SchedulerTaskProvider adapter. The adapter must reproduce the exact rowToTask/taskToRow transformation so the golden-master passes post-extraction.
src/db.js singleton (line 12 of runSchedule.js, line 30 of scheduleQueue.js, line 21 of schedulerSession.js) must all migrate to lib-db in their respective adapters.
reconcileOccurrences.js (126 ln) and scheduleQueue.js (368 ln) contain I/O that must stay in the adapter/infrastructure layer — they cannot be part of the pure domain core.
The cache path in runSchedule.js (getSchedulePlacements) uses Redis and user_config cache rows — this is infrastructure that belongs in an adapter, not RunScheduleCommand.
schedulerSession.js (281 ln) manages session state in DB — this is pure infrastructure and must stay outside the domain core.
The in-memory chunk expansion path (desiredOccurrences, toInsertIds, toDeleteIds) in runSchedule.js is I/O — it must live in the adapter layer.
The deadlock-retry loop (runScheduleAndPersist retries up to MAX_RETRIES=3 on ER_LOCK_DEADLOCK) is infrastructure — must stay in RunScheduleCommand or KnexScheduleRepository, not the pure core.
The rolling-anchor backfill path (lines 389-403 of runSchedule.js, using trx.fn.now()) is a write path that must migrate to new Date() in the adapter.
The sync-lock (lib/sync-lock) claim is infra — must stay in the RunScheduleCommand orchestrator, not leak into the pure core.

recommended_routing.rationale: Measured: 5,356 ln across 13 source files + 5,763 ln of existing tests. 19 ADR-0003 violations found by reading (not greppable). Zero scheduler golden-master characterization exists — S8 gate is a hard BLOCK. Three structural seams to cut (task.controller dependency, db.js singleton, fn.now() violations). HIGHEST-risk per DESIGN §6 and CLAUDE.md ('scheduler bugs cascade and corrupt all task data'). Full+deep is the only appropriate routing — no part of this refactor is trivial.
